text: Hello everyone. This is a video presentation of local excision for rectal cancer performed using DaVinci X robotic platform. This is a seventy nine year old female who was referred with change in bowel habits. Her endoscopy revealed a three centimeter lesion with central depression, which was about seven centimeters from the anal margin. The endorectal ultrasound scan revealed this to be a T one and not lesion. The patient had a BMI of thirty and her comorbidities included hypertension and hiatus hernia. The MRI revealed a posterior lesion. In the sagittal view, you can see it's almost next to the sphincter complex.

I'm going to briefly talk about our theatre setup as this is a crucial step for the robotic platform. The patient's legs are placed in the stirrups. They are extended and abducted so as to avoid the clash with robotic arms. The patient's side guard comes from the patient's left shoulder. We use a gel point port. The three ports are used by the robotic arms and the fourth port is an air seal port, which is used by the assistant to control the suction during the surgery. This picture shows our setup of robotic platform. The camera is at the top with robotic instruments on either side. An eight millimeter air seal port is used for interpolation and as an assistant port.

The surgeon marks the margins circumferentially around the rectal lesion, leaving about a centimeter from the lesion. A full thickness excision is performed up to the mesorectum, revealing the yellow mesorectal fat. The dissection continues circumferentially using scissors and fenestrated bipolar forceps, ensuring careful handling of the lesion. After the lesion is fully dissected, the area is washed, hemostasis is checked, and the defect is closed with a V-lock suture.
The specimen is visually fixed onto a polystyrene board, preparing it for pathology analysis.
